capabilities.description: Clarify goals, affected subjects, constraints, vocabulary, and acceptance boundaries before solution work.
fallback: Record assumptions and stop decisions that depend on unanswered questions.
validation: Confirm unresolved questions and acceptance boundaries with the responsible subject.

capabilities.description: Turn an agreed problem into a reviewable product or technical decision document.
fallback: Keep the document in draft and identify missing decision authority.
validation: Review scope, outcomes, constraints, alternatives, and acceptance evidence.

capabilities.description: Decompose an approved outcome into bounded, independently verifiable work slices.
fallback: Retain the larger outcome and mark decomposition gaps instead of inventing tasks.
validation: Check that each slice has inputs, outputs, dependencies, and observable completion.

capabilities.description: Design system boundaries, responsibilities, interactions, and evolution constraints.
fallback: Use the smallest reversible boundary change and document uncertainty.
validation: Review the design against current domain language, decisions, and operational constraints.

capabilities.description: Design stable interfaces and contracts between components or subjects.
fallback: Expose the narrowest reversible interface and defer unsupported flexibility.
validation: Evaluate alternatives against callers, failure modes, compatibility, and testability.

capabilities.description: Model data entities, invariants, ownership, retention, and change semantics.
fallback: Compose requirements and architecture modeling, then avoid irreversible schema commitments until accountable review.
validation: Require domain and storage experts to review invariants and lifecycle assumptions.

capabilities.description: Design accessible, usable, responsive user-facing interactions and visual structure.
fallback: Provide an implementation-neutral interaction specification and preserve design uncertainty.
validation: Probe the live environment for an authorized design workflow and verify the rendered result.

capabilities.description: Implement server-side behavior, integrations, persistence boundaries, and failure handling.
fallback: Implement a smaller reversible slice and report unavailable execution support.
validation: Run repository-defined tests, static checks, and contract verification.

capabilities.description: Develop behavior through observable red, green, and refactor cycles.
fallback: Add the smallest reproducible regression test before changing behavior.
validation: Capture a failing test before the minimal implementation and rerun it after refactoring.

capabilities.description: Choose risk-proportionate test layers, evidence, environments, and quality gates.
fallback: Prioritize critical-path integration checks and disclose untested risks.
validation: Trace each material risk to an executable check and an evidence owner.

capabilities.description: Review changes for correctness, standards, maintainability, and unintended effects.
fallback: Narrow the review scope and state which change surfaces remain unreviewed.
validation: Inspect the bounded change set and verify actionable findings against repository evidence.

capabilities.description: Assess trust boundaries, attack paths, vulnerabilities, and mitigation evidence.
fallback: Perform a bounded manual threat review and recommend specialist review for unresolved risk.
validation: Probe for an authorized security workflow and validate findings from source to impact.

capabilities.description: Determine lawful purpose, minimization, access, retention, and accountable handling of sensitive data.
fallback: Minimize collection and pause processing that lacks a valid authority decision.
validation: Obtain approval from the accountable privacy or legal authority before consequential processing.

capabilities.description: Define budgets and measure latency, throughput, responsiveness, and resource use.
fallback: Report the measurement boundary and avoid unsupported performance claims.
validation: Compare reproducible measurements with an explicit budget or baseline.

capabilities.description: Design actionable logs, metrics, traces, alerts, and service-level evidence.
fallback: Add bounded diagnostic evidence for the critical path and document blind spots.
validation: Verify that critical behaviors and failures are visible without exposing restricted data.

capabilities.description: Automate repeatable integration, quality gates, packaging, and staged delivery.
fallback: Provide a reproducible manual gate sequence without claiming automated delivery.
validation: Run the delivery workflow in its governed environment and inspect gate evidence.

capabilities.description: Make a bounded release decision from readiness, rollout, monitoring, and rollback evidence.
fallback: Hold the release and identify the smallest missing readiness evidence.
validation: Confirm explicit go or no-go authority, acceptance evidence, monitoring, and recovery readiness.

capabilities.description: Replace or retire systems and contracts through measured compatibility and adoption stages.
fallback: Preserve compatibility and delay irreversible removal until evidence is sufficient.
validation: Verify compatibility, adoption, removal gates, and affected-subject communication.

capabilities.description: Restore a known-safe state or service objective after a failed change or incident.
fallback: Stop further change, contain impact, and escalate to the accountable operator.
validation: Exercise the recovery path and verify state integrity plus restored service objectives.

capabilities.description: Reproduce, isolate, explain, and regression-test faults or unexpected behavior.
fallback: Minimize the reproduction and report competing hypotheses with evidence gaps.
validation: Confirm the causal explanation with instrumentation and a passing regression test.

capabilities.description: Classify incoming work by impact, urgency, evidence, ownership, and next state.
fallback: Keep the issue uncommitted to a solution and request missing evidence.
validation: Confirm classification and ownership against the issue lifecycle policy.

capabilities.description: Keep authoritative documentation aligned with domain language, decisions, and implementation evidence.
fallback: Mark stale or uncertain statements and retain traceable source references.
validation: Cross-check updated documentation with authoritative repository and decision records.

capabilities.description: Capture reusable, source-linked learning without promoting transient state to authority.
fallback: Keep a local evidence note and defer durable publication until reviewed.
validation: Review captured knowledge for provenance, sensitivity, freshness, and retrieval value.

capabilities.description: Identify, classify, prioritize, and retire structural liabilities with bounded value evidence.
fallback: Record the debt with impact and defer mutation until authority and capacity exist.
validation: Tie proposed debt work to observable risk, cost, ownership, and verification.

capabilities.description: Transfer bounded context, evidence, decisions, and next actions between collaborating agents or subjects.
fallback: Provide a minimal evidence-linked status summary and request receiver confirmation.
validation: Verify that the receiver can identify scope, current truth, uncertainties, and allowed next actions.

capabilities.description: Turn outcome feedback into validated improvements to decisions, workflows, and safeguards.
fallback: Record observed outcomes and unresolved causes for later accountable review.
validation: Compare intended and actual outcomes, then assign measurable follow-up changes.

evidenceGates: Boundary goals, callers, failure modes, compatibility expectations, and test surfaces are identified.
At least one candidate contract is reviewed against architecture and code-review capabilities.
failurePolicy: If callers, compatibility needs, or failure modes are unknown, stop at requirements clarification.
If implementation evidence is unavailable, publish only a reversible interface proposal.

steps.when: a candidate contract or implementation exists

terminalCriteria: A narrow contract, known tradeoffs, verification surface, and unresolved assumptions are documented.
recipes.trigger: A component boundary or API needs alternatives evaluated without a dedicated duplicate Skill

recipes.authorization: Irreversible schema, migration, retention, and regulated-data decisions remain subject to their accountable human authorities.
evidenceGates: Entities, invariants, ownership, retention, lifecycle semantics, and change risks are named.
Privacy or regulated-data implications are evaluated when the model touches sensitive data.
failurePolicy: If authority or data sensitivity is unclear, stop before schema, migration, or retention commitments.
If no reviewable schema or contract exists, keep the output as a model proposal.

steps.when: personal, confidential, regulated, or retention-sensitive data is involved
a schema, contract, or migration candidate exists

terminalCriteria: The model has reviewable invariants, owners, retention assumptions, and reversible next steps.
recipes.trigger: A system needs explicit entities, invariants, ownership, retention, and change semantics without adding an overlapping dedicated Skill

evidenceGates: Material risks are mapped to test layers, owners, commands, environments, and acceptance evidence.
Performance, security, and delivery gates are included when their risk conditions apply.
failurePolicy: If risk ownership or execution environment is missing, identify the unverified risks instead of claiming coverage.
If an automated gate is unavailable, provide the smallest reproducible manual check.

steps.when: behavior is being added or corrected
a performance objective or regression risk exists
a trust boundary or material threat changes

terminalCriteria: Each critical risk has an executable or explicitly deferred check with a stated evidence owner.
recipes.trigger: A change needs risk-proportionate verification across test layers and delivery gates

evidenceGates: Purpose, minimization, redaction, retention, access, and trust-boundary assumptions are explicit.
Telemetry fields and destinations are reviewed before collection or export.
failurePolicy: If privacy authority or data classification is missing, stop before adding or exporting telemetry.
If security review is unavailable for a new boundary, keep evidence local and bounded.

steps.when: purpose, minimization, redaction, retention, and access are defined
telemetry crosses a new trust boundary

terminalCriteria: Telemetry captures the critical behavior while minimizing sensitive exposure and documenting blind spots.
recipes.trigger: Operational evidence may contain personal, confidential, regulated, or secret data

recipes.authorization: Every merge, publication, release, deployment, migration, or rollback remains separately authorized.
evidenceGates: Test, observability, performance, delivery, rollback, and release-readiness evidence are available or explicitly waived.
Go or no-go authority and affected-subject communication path are identified.
failurePolicy: If release authority or rollback evidence is missing, hold the release.
If any gate is unavailable, record the smallest missing evidence and do not imply readiness.
terminalCriteria: A go, no-go, or hold decision is recorded with readiness evidence, monitoring, and recovery posture.
recipes.trigger: A change is approaching a governed release boundary

evidenceGates: Compatibility, adoption, observability, rollback, and release-readiness evidence are present.
Affected consumers and removal gates are named.
failurePolicy: If consumers or rollback path are unknown, preserve compatibility and defer removal.
If adoption evidence is incomplete, continue migration support rather than cutting over.
terminalCriteria: Migration can proceed, pause, or retire only with documented adoption, compatibility, and rollback state.
recipes.trigger: An interface, dependency, feature, data contract, or system is replaced or retired

recipes.authorization: Production rollback and destructive recovery actions require the accountable operator's explicit authorization.
evidenceGates: Current impact, known-safe target, observability signals, and accountable operator are identified.
Regression evidence is planned when recovery behavior changes.
failurePolicy: If operator authority is missing, contain impact and stop before destructive recovery.
If the safe target is unknown, preserve evidence and escalate instead of guessing.

steps.when: safe restoration does not explain the cause

terminalCriteria: Service or state objective is restored, contained, or explicitly escalated with remaining risk recorded.
recipes.trigger: A failed change or incident requires controlled restoration of a known-safe state

evidenceGates: Learning is tied to source evidence, sensitivity review, freshness, and expected retrieval value.
Authoritative documentation changes are separated from local notes and handoff material.
failurePolicy: If provenance or sensitivity is unclear, keep the note local and do not promote it to authority.
If no future consumer is known, capture a minimal source-linked summary instead of expanding process.

steps.when: learning changes an authoritative standard or operating path
another subject will continue the work

terminalCriteria: Reusable learning is captured, deliberately deferred, or handed off with provenance and authority boundaries.
recipes.trigger: A completed decision, delivery, incident, or experiment produced reusable learning

relations.condition: behavior must be measurable before optimization
a release changes or removes an existing contract
an issue is accepted as a reproducible fault

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Repository delivery target, test commands, environments, and deployment constraints are available.
fallback: Use native planning for a read-only pipeline proposal; use a visible provider diagnostic capability for failing runs.
humanConfirmWhen: Before writing CI configuration, changing secrets, publishing, or deploying.
inputs: Repository delivery requirements and current automation.
negativeTriggers: A currently failing hosted CI run needs provider-specific diagnosis rather than pipeline design.
outputs: Reviewed pipeline design or configuration with gates and rollback.
permissionsRequired: Repository write; deployment access only when explicitly authorized.
positiveTriggers: Design or change an automated delivery pipeline, quality gate, staged deployment, or release automation.
sideEffects: May change repository CI configuration after authorization.
validation: Pipeline syntax and representative gates run successfully in the target environment.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Current and target contracts, consumers, compatibility window, and rollback constraints are known.
fallback: Keep the old contract active and produce a read-only migration proposal.
humanConfirmWhen: Before destructive migration, data rewrite, removal, or irreversible cutoff.
inputs: Current contract, target contract, consumers, risks, and timing.
negativeTriggers: A new isolated feature with no compatibility or removal path does not need migration governance.
outputs: Phased migration and deprecation plan with compatibility, validation, and rollback.
permissionsRequired: Read access for planning; write/migration authority only when explicit.
positiveTriggers: Replace, consolidate, retire, or migrate an API, dependency, feature, system, or data contract.
sideEffects: May propose or execute state/data changes only after separate authorization.
validation: Compatibility tests, observability, adoption evidence, and rollback criteria close.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Observed symptom, reproduction attempt, expected behavior, and relevant environment are available.
fallback: Narrow uncertainty and request the missing reproduction or evidence.
humanConfirmWhen: Before production mutation, broad instrumentation, data access, or destructive diagnostics.
inputs: Symptoms, logs, reproduction, recent changes, and expected behavior.
negativeTriggers: A known implementation task with no failure symptom should use normal coding or TDD.
outputs: Minimal reproduction, evidence-backed cause, fix boundary, and regression test.
permissionsRequired: Read access by default; write or production instrumentation requires authorization.
positiveTriggers: A bug, failure, intermittent behavior, or unexplained performance regression needs root-cause evidence.
sideEffects: May add temporary instrumentation or change code after authorization.
validation: Original symptom reproduces before the fix and the regression test passes after it.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Target scope, protected commands, shell environment, and hook ownership are known.
fallback: Provide a read-only guardrail design and manual commands without installing.
humanConfirmWhen: Before installing, replacing, or broadening a persistent hook.
inputs: Desired Git safety policy and target repository or user scope.
negativeTriggers: A one-time ordinary Git command does not justify installing persistent guardrails.
outputs: Reviewed guardrail hook/configuration and verification evidence.
permissionsRequired: Explicit permission to modify hooks or user/repository configuration.
positiveTriggers: Prevent destructive Git commands or install repository/user-level Git safety hooks.
sideEffects: Installs or changes Git hooks or command policy.
validation: Blocked commands fail safely while allowed commands continue to work.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Relevant domain documents and the decision under review are available.
fallback: Run a read-only challenge and return proposed document changes.
humanConfirmWhen: Before changing authoritative domain or architecture decisions.
inputs: Plan or design plus CONTEXT, ADR, glossary, or equivalent domain records.
negativeTriggers: A casual question or plan without a documentation/domain context can use native discussion.
outputs: Resolved ambiguities and reviewed updates to domain/decision documents.
permissionsRequired: Read domain documents; write permission for documentation updates.
positiveTriggers: Stress-test a plan against existing domain language, ADRs, constraints, and documented decisions.
sideEffects: May update documentation only after the user approves the evolving decisions.
validation: Terms, decisions, unresolved branches, and document changes are internally consistent.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Current goal, repository state, decisions, evidence, constraints, and next actions are known.
fallback: Return the handoff in chat without writing a file.
humanConfirmWhen: Before including sensitive context or writing outside the requested repository.
inputs: Current work state and authoritative continuation sources.
negativeTriggers: A normal summary with no continuation consumer does not need a handoff artifact.
outputs: Compact handoff artifact with verified state, constraints, and next actions.
permissionsRequired: Write permission only when persisting the handoff.
positiveTriggers: Another Agent or future session must continue work from durable evidence.
sideEffects: Writes a handoff document when requested.
validation: A fresh Agent can locate authority, reproduce posture, and identify the next bounded action.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Repository structure plus domain language or decision records are available.
fallback: Return a bounded architecture assessment with explicit unknowns.
humanConfirmWhen: Before broad refactoring, module moves, public contract changes, or migrations.
inputs: Codebase, domain context, ADRs, constraints, and quality goals.
negativeTriggers: A small local refactor with known scope can use native coding and tests.
outputs: Evidence-backed architecture opportunities with boundaries and validation.
permissionsRequired: Repository read; writes only when separately authorized.
positiveTriggers: Find architecture or refactoring opportunities grounded in domain language and existing decisions.
sideEffects: Read-only by default; implementation changes require a separate task.
validation: Recommendations trace to code evidence, domain concepts, and measurable validation.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: TypeScript test files, current assertion pattern, and target type contracts are available.
fallback: Explain the type-safe transformation without modifying files.
humanConfirmWhen: Before adding a dependency or mechanically rewriting a broad test surface.
negativeTriggers: Production type assertions or non-TypeScript tests require another technique.
outputs: Type-safe test-data construction with preserved intent and autocomplete.
permissionsRequired: Repository write and dependency change only when authorized.
positiveTriggers: TypeScript tests use unsafe type assertions and need type-safe partial or intentionally invalid test data.
sideEffects: Changes test code and possibly a test-only dependency after authorization.
validation: Type checking and affected tests pass without unsafe assertions.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: System boundaries, failure modes, data sensitivity, operators, and signal consumers are known.
fallback: Produce a privacy-aware read-only instrumentation proposal.
humanConfirmWhen: Before collecting new data, changing retention/access, or connecting an external telemetry service.
inputs: Architecture, operational questions, privacy constraints, and existing signals.
negativeTriggers: A fully reproduced local bug with adequate evidence should use diagnosis rather than broad telemetry.
outputs: Signal design, instrumentation changes, dashboards/alerts, and validation plan.
permissionsRequired: Code/config write and telemetry-service access when explicitly authorized.
positiveTriggers: Production behavior lacks sufficient logs, metrics, traces, alerts, SLOs, or async-job visibility.
sideEffects: May change telemetry collection and external data flow after authorization.
validation: Signals answer defined operational questions without leaking sensitive data or creating noise.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Baseline measurement, target/budget, workload, and representative environment are available.
fallback: Define measurement and budget before proposing code changes.
humanConfirmWhen: Before production load, costly compute, broad caching, or architectural trade-offs.
inputs: Metrics, profiles, workload, budget, and suspected bottlenecks.
negativeTriggers: No measurement, budget, regression, or bottleneck exists; speculative optimization should not trigger.
outputs: Measured experiment, bounded optimization, and regression evidence.
permissionsRequired: Repository write; load/production access only when explicit.
positiveTriggers: A measured latency, throughput, resource, scalability, or responsiveness target needs diagnosis or optimization.
sideEffects: May change code/config or run load tests after authorization.
validation: Representative measurement improves without violating correctness or other budgets.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Question to answer, throwaway boundary, timebox, and success/discard criteria are known.
fallback: Use sketches, pseudocode, or a state table when runnable code is unnecessary.
humanConfirmWhen: Before external deployment, paid services, or promoting prototype code to production.
inputs: Uncertainty, constraints, representative inputs, and desired interaction.
negativeTriggers: Requirements and production design are already settled and implementation should be durable.
outputs: Disposable runnable prototype and evidence answering the stated question.
permissionsRequired: Write permission for an isolated prototype; external services require separate authorization.
positiveTriggers: A design, state model, interaction, or technical assumption needs a disposable runnable experiment.
sideEffects: Creates temporary code/assets in an isolated scope.
validation: The prototype answers the question and is clearly separated from production code.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Fixed comparison point, repository standards, and originating spec/issue are available.
fallback: Review the supplied patch and state missing standards/spec context.
humanConfirmWhen: Before posting review comments or changing code.
inputs: Git diff, standards, spec, tests, and relevant evidence.
negativeTriggers: A repository-wide security audit or an unfixed known bug needs its specialized workflow.
outputs: Prioritized findings separated by standards and spec compliance.
positiveTriggers: Changes since a fixed Git point need independent standards and specification compliance review.
sideEffects: Read-only unless the user separately asks to fix findings.
validation: Every finding cites evidence and the review base is explicit and reproducible.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Repository authority, issue tracker, label vocabulary, and domain-document locations are known.
fallback: Return the proposed context block without writing.
humanConfirmWhen: Before changing authoritative Agent instructions or project workflow vocabulary.
inputs: Repository instructions and project workflow metadata.
negativeTriggers: The repository already exposes a verified current Agent context contract.
outputs: Neutral project Agent-skills context block and supporting documentation.
positiveTriggers: Engineering Skills lack repository-specific issue-tracker, triage, or domain-document context.
sideEffects: Writes repository Agent instructions and docs.
validation: Engineering Skills resolve project context without author, device, or Agent-specific assumptions.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Scope, owners, tests, observability, risk, rollout, rollback, and acceptance evidence are available.
fallback: Return NO-GO with missing evidence and a closure plan.
humanConfirmWhen: Before every publish, deploy, migration, traffic shift, or rollback.
inputs: Release candidate, readiness evidence, operational plan, and business constraints.
negativeTriggers: Ordinary Git publication without production readiness decisions should use normal version control.
outputs: GO/NO-GO decision package, rollout, monitoring, rollback, and post-launch checks.
permissionsRequired: Read for assessment; publish/deploy/migration authority only when explicit.
positiveTriggers: A product or service change is approaching production, beta, staged rollout, migration, or launch decision.
sideEffects: May publish, deploy, migrate, or roll back only under separate explicit authorization.
validation: All gates have evidence and rollback/monitoring are executable.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Desired behavior, runnable test command, and writable implementation scope are known.
fallback: Define the test contract and command when execution is unavailable.
humanConfirmWhen: Before destructive setup changes, dependency installation, or scope expansion.
inputs: Behavior contract, test harness, and current implementation boundary.
negativeTriggers: Read-only explanation, generated artifacts, or configuration-only changes explicitly exempted by the user.
outputs: Failing test evidence, minimal implementation, passing suite, and refactored result.
positiveTriggers: A feature or bug fix should be developed test-first with observable red-green-refactor evidence.
sideEffects: Changes tests and production code within the authorized scope.
validation: The new test fails for the expected reason before implementation and passes afterward with the full suite.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Approved source plan, issue tracker, project conventions, and acceptance boundaries are available.
fallback: Produce local issue drafts without publishing.
humanConfirmWhen: Before creating or modifying external issues.
inputs: Plan/spec, dependencies, tracer-bullet path, and issue conventions.
negativeTriggers: The plan is still ambiguous or no issue tracker/output authority is known.
outputs: Ordered vertical-slice issues with scope, acceptance, dependencies, and validation.
permissionsRequired: Read by default; issue-tracker write only when explicit.
positiveTriggers: An approved plan, spec, or PRD must become independently grabbable vertical-slice issues.
sideEffects: May create external issues only after authorization.
validation: Each issue is independently actionable and the set closes the source plan without horizontal-layer gaps.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Problem, users, outcomes, constraints, scope, and publication target are sufficiently known.
fallback: Return a local PRD draft and unresolved questions.
humanConfirmWhen: Before publishing or treating assumptions as approved requirements.
inputs: Conversation decisions, evidence, users, constraints, and success criteria.
negativeTriggers: A technical implementation plan with settled product intent should use planning/decomposition instead.
outputs: PRD with problem, goals, non-goals, requirements, risks, and acceptance.
permissionsRequired: Read context; external publication only when explicit.
positiveTriggers: Conversation context must be converted into a reviewable product requirements document.
sideEffects: May publish to an issue tracker only after authorization.
validation: The PRD separates facts/assumptions, resolves scope, and has testable acceptance criteria.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Issue data, state model, labels, ownership rules, and evidence are available.
fallback: Return a local triage recommendation without changing external state.
humanConfirmWhen: Before changing external issue state, labels, owners, or closing work.
inputs: Issues, reports, project triage policy, and current state.
negativeTriggers: A single known implementation task already has accepted scope and owner.
outputs: Evidence-backed state transitions, labels, owners, and next actions.
permissionsRequired: Read by default; issue-tracker write only when explicit.
positiveTriggers: Issues, bugs, or feature requests need state, priority, ownership, readiness, or next-action classification.
sideEffects: May update issue trackers only after authorization.
validation: Every transition follows the declared state machine and unresolved evidence remains visible.

agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime
contextRequirements: Conversation/project domain evidence and intended glossary authority are available.
fallback: Return the glossary proposal in chat.
inputs: Domain statements, documents, conflicting terms, and affected contexts.
negativeTriggers: A simple dictionary definition with no domain decisions can use native explanation.
outputs: Ubiquitous-language glossary with ambiguities, canonical terms, and rejected alternatives.
permissionsRequired: Read context; write permission for glossary updates.
positiveTriggers: Domain terms are ambiguous, inconsistent, overloaded, or need a canonical glossary.
sideEffects: May write or update a domain glossary after authorization.
validation: Terms are grounded in evidence, distinctions are explicit, and unresolved ambiguity remains marked.
